# payba3 LLM Index

payba3 is a developer-first integration surface for multiple payment and verification providers. It is designed for apps, libraries, backend services, and AI agents that need one predictable way to choose a provider and call provider-specific capabilities.

## Provider References

- Paystack: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/paystack.txt` or `src/lib/paystack/paystack_llm.txt`
- Safehaven: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/safehaven.txt` or `src/lib/safehaven/safehaven_llm.txt`
- SeerBit: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/seerbit.txt` or `src/lib/seerbit/seerbit_llm.txt`
- OPay: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/opay.txt` or `src/lib/opay/opay_llm.txt`
- Mono: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/mono.txt` or `src/lib/mono/mono_llm.txt`
- Monnify: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/monnify.txt` or `src/lib/monnify/monnify_llm.txt`
- QoreID: `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/qoreid.txt` or `src/lib/qoreid/qoreid_llm.txt`

## Integration Rules

- Import payba3 from `@grtsnx/payba3` and select providers with `Payba3Service.use('<provider>')`.
- Configure only the provider you use.
- Use provider-specific environment switches such as `SAFEHAVEN_ENVIRONMENT`, `OPAY_ENVIRONMENT`, `MONNIFY_ENVIRONMENT`, `PAYSTACK_ENVIRONMENT`, `QOREID_ENVIRONMENT`, and `SEERBIT_ENVIRONMENT`.
- Keep secrets server-side.
- Verify provider webhooks before trusting callback payloads.
- Verify transaction status server-side before delivering value.
- Refresh expiring access tokens before they expire.
- payba3 is Nest-native in `1.x`; core Nest packages are peer dependencies to avoid duplicate Nest runtimes.
- Most package managers install peer dependencies automatically; manually install `@nestjs/common`, `@nestjs/core`, `@nestjs/config`, `reflect-metadata`, and `rxjs` only when the host toolchain asks for them.
- For IDE and AI-agent use, read `@grtsnx/payba3/llms.txt`, then read the provider-specific `@grtsnx/payba3/llms/<provider>.txt` file before generating integration code.
- Agents must generate server-side code only, keep secrets in environment variables, and never expose provider keys to client-side code.
- Validate package readiness with `bun run pack:dry`, `bun run test:package`, and `bun run test:package:bun`.
